Implements the RangeError prototype for ECMAScript built-in objects in the JerryScript engine.

# Purpose
This C source code file is part of the ECMAScript engine implementation, specifically dealing with the built-in `RangeError` prototype object. It includes various headers that provide necessary functions and macros for memory allocation, garbage collection, exception handling, and object manipulation within the ECMAScript environment. The file conditionally includes internal routines for the `RangeError` prototype if error built-ins are not disabled, using a template mechanism defined in `ecma-builtin-internal-routines-template.inc.h`. This setup allows the engine to handle `RangeError` objects according to the ECMAScript specification, facilitating error management in JavaScript execution.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `ecma-alloc.h`
- `ecma-builtins.h`
- `ecma-conversion.h`
- `ecma-exceptions.h`
- `ecma-gc.h`
- `ecma-globals.h`
- `ecma-helpers.h`
- `ecma-objects.h`
- `ecma-string-object.h`
- `ecma-try-catch-macro.h`
- `jrt.h`
- `ecma-builtins-internal.h`
- `ecma-builtin-internal-routines-template.inc.h`
